description: FutSpring Chat — real-time group chat per pelada using WebSocket (STOMP over SockJS), persistent message history, and JWT auth on WebSocket handshake

userStories.title: Backend Message entity, WebSocket config, JWT auth, and REST history endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend WebSocket infrastructure and message persistence so the frontend can send/receive chat messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Message JPA entity with fields: id (Long, auto), pelada (ManyToOne Pelada, required), sender (ManyToOne User, required), content (String, not blank, max 500), sentAt (LocalDateTime, @CreationTimestamp)
Create MessageRepository extending JpaRepository<Message, Long> with: findByPeladaOrderBySentAtDesc(Pelada pelada, Pageable pageable)

Configure WebSocket: @EnableWebSocketMessageBroker; WebSocketMessageBrokerConfigurer implementation; registerStompEndpoints at '/ws' with SockJS fallback and setAllowedOriginPatterns('*'); configureMessageBroker with in-memory broker on '/topic' and application destination prefix '/app'

JWT auth on WebSocket: implement ChannelInterceptor (not HandshakeInterceptor); in preSend() for CONNECT frames, extract JWT from nativeHeaders 'Authorization' header (strip 'Bearer '), validate via JwtTokenProvider, set Authentication in SecurityContextHolder and in the accessor; register the interceptor in configureClientInboundChannel()
Create MessageDTO with fields: id, content, sentAt (String ISO), sender (nested SenderDTO: id, username, image); static MessageDTO.from(Message m) factory

Create ChatController: @MessageMapping('/pelada/{peladaId}/send') handler — validates sender is a pelada member (403 otherwise), validates content is not blank and <= 500 chars (400 otherwise), persists Message, broadcasts MessageDTO to '/topic/pelada/{peladaId}' via SimpMessagingTemplate; method signature: public void sendMessage(@DestinationVariable Long peladaId, @Payload SendMessageRequest request, Principal principal)
Create SendMessageRequest DTO with: content (String)

GET /api/v1/peladas/:id/messages?page=0&size=50 — members only (403); returns List<MessageDTO> for the pelada sorted by sentAt ascending (reverse the desc query result); uses Pageable with page/size params; add to PeladaController or new MessageController
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend chat sidebar with message history
userStories.description: As a member, I want to see past messages when I open the pelada page so I can catch up on the conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/types/chat.ts with interfaces: SenderDTO { id, username, image }, MessageDTO { id, content, sentAt, sender: SenderDTO }
Create src/api/chat.ts with getChatHistory(peladaId, page?, size?) using existing Axios client
Add a Chat sidebar to PeladaDetailPage on the right side; on desktop it is visible alongside the main content; on mobile it is accessible but can be collapsed
Chat sidebar fetches getChatHistory(peladaId) on mount; shows last 50 messages sorted oldest-to-newest (ascending sentAt)
Each message shows: sender avatar (PlayerAvatar or initials fallback), sender username, message content, timestamp (relative: '2 min ago' or absolute on hover using date-fns or inline calculation)
My own messages (match useAuth().user.id to sender.id) appear right-aligned with a distinct background (e.g. blue); others appear left-aligned
Chat scrolled to bottom on initial load
Skeleton loading state (5 skeleton message rows) while fetching
Empty state: 'No messages yet. Say hi!'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend STOMP client — send message and receive in real-time
userStories.description: As a member, I want to send messages and see new messages from others in real-time without refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @stomp/stompjs and sockjs-client packages (npm install @stomp/stompjs sockjs-client) and their types (@types/sockjs-client)
On PeladaDetailPage mount, create a STOMP client connecting to '/ws' with SockJS; pass JWT in connectHeaders: { Authorization: 'Bearer <token>' }; get token from useAuth() or localStorage
Subscribe to '/topic/pelada/{peladaId}' on connect; on message received: parse MessageDTO from frame body and append to messages list
Text input at the bottom of the chat sidebar; Send button (or Enter key) publishes to '/app/pelada/{peladaId}/send' with body { content }
Input cleared after sending; message content trimmed before send; empty/whitespace-only input not sent
New messages trigger auto-scroll to bottom if the user is already within 100px of the bottom (check scrollTop + clientHeight vs scrollHeight)
On component unmount, deactivate the STOMP client
Toast shown on publish error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend WebSocket reconnection with backoff and indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want the chat to reconnect automatically after a brief disconnect so I don't miss messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Configure STOMP client reconnectDelay; use exponential backoff: attempt 1 = 1s, attempt 2 = 2s, attempt 3 = 4s; max 3 retries then stop
Track connection state (connected | reconnecting | failed) in component state
Show a subtle banner/badge 'Reconnecting...' inside the chat sidebar when state is 'reconnecting'
Show 'Connection failed. Refresh to retry.' when state is 'failed' (max retries exhausted)
On successful reconnect, re-subscribe to the topic and clear the indicator
Typecheck passes
